question.text: An acquirer currently holds 28% voting rights in a listed company. Under SEBI SAST Regulations 2011, what is the maximum additional voting rights the acquirer can acquire in any financial year without triggering an open offer?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 3 of SAST 2011, an acquirer who already holds 25% or more (but less than the maximum non-public shareholding) cannot acquire more than 5% additional voting rights in any financial year without triggering a mandatory open offer. Since the acquirer holds 28% (above 25%), the 5% creeping acquisition limit applies. **The Trap:** "No further acquisition is permitted" is wrong — creeping acquisitions up to 5% per year are allowed. The 10% option confuses this with other thresholds. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XV (Research Analyst) tests knowledge of shareholding patterns and disclosure requirements that interact with SAST creeping acquisition limits. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Track gross acquisitions, not net — SAST counts total shares acquired regardless of any intermittent sales or dilution during the year.
